task: VOID-EVERYWHERE PR-4 (gated, default OFF, NO migration). Adds void to Expenses. Per the locked answer, expenses do NOT post to the GL, so the void is the CLEAN case: a pure status flip + permission + required reason + audit, with NO reversing journal entry (unlike bills/invoices in PR-1/PR-2). New endpoint POST /api/v1/expenses/:expenseId/void in expenses.routes.ts: gated behind VOID_ENFORCEMENT_ENABLED (isVoidEnforcementEnabled) -- when OFF the endpoint is inert (409 void_not_enabled, no behaviour change in prod); when ON it enforces VOID = Owner+Accountant (canVoid, Administrator excluded), requires a reason, idempotency-guards (409 expense_already_void), flips accounting.expenses.status to 'void' (updated_at set defensively via columnExists since the table is adaptively shaped), and writes the void to the audit spine (appendCrudAudit accounting.expenses.voided, severity warning -- the canonical who/what/when/reason record). NO reversing JE, NO postVoidReversal, NO GL read, NO VoidableEntityType change. No migration: status column already exists; reason/actor/timestamp live in the audit spine, not new expense columns. Reuses canVoid + isVoidEnforcementEnabled from void.service.ts (PR-1). Gated financial -- show Jorge the money-path diff; never self-merge. Does NOT touch advances (PR-3, needs block-if-linked design) or settlements.
